title: Recursive binary protocol parser with multi-clause heads
prompt: Write lib/my_app/protocol/parser.ex (module MyApp.Protocol.Parser). It parses a byte stream for a simple fictional protocol: start with 4-byte magic "MPRO", then a 1-byte version (0x01), then a 2-byte message type (big-endian uint16), then a 4-byte payload length (big-endian uint32), then the payload bytes. Define parse/1 that takes a binary and returns {:ok, %{version: v, type: t, payload: payload}} | {:error, reason}. Use multi-clause function heads with binary pattern matching — NOT String.starts_with? or byte_size. The function heads: parse/1 dispatches on the magic bytes prefix pattern. A private helper parse_header/1 pattern-matches on the version+type+length prefix and extracts the payload. A private helper extract_payload/2 pattern-matches on <<payload::binary-size(len), rest::binary>> to slice exactly len bytes. If any stage fails to match, return {:error, :malformed} (not raise).
tier_rationale: Requires knowledge of Elixir binary pattern syntax (::binary-size, ::unsigned-big-integer-size) AND the discipline to dispatch via function heads rather than imperative slicing. Sonnet usually reaches for String.slice or byte_size loops.
